title: Misidentification + contamination audit vs. ICLAC + Cellosaurus
context: Cell-line misidentification and cross-contamination are pervasive in preclinical cancer research (the reason ICLAC exists). Every catalogued model must be audited against the ICLAC register and Cellosaurus problematic flags, with the status recorded as a provenanced field and prominent warnings where applicable. Standing guardrails bind this task: open-access sources only (no dbGaP/EGA/biobank/controlled-access); COSMIC/OncoKB are link-only; donor fields are published-only with no re-identification or external linkage; every assertion carries field-level provenance; the deliverable is de-identified research metadata, never patient data and never a clinical/therapeutic recommendation.
objective: Audit every catalogued model for misidentification/contamination against ICLAC + Cellosaurus and record the provenanced status.
acceptanceCriteria: Every catalogued model checked against the ICLAC register (version recorded) and Cellosaurus 'problematic' flags; status recorded as a provenanced field.
Any model found misidentified/contaminated carries a prominent, sourced warning in its entry.
Findings that contradict an upstream record are routed to upstream-012.
resources: ICLAC Register of Misidentified Cell Lines
output: MISIDENTIFICATION-AUDIT.md plus per-entry provenanced authentication-status fields.
